pr.title: Create script for finding easy functions
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Added tools/easy_funcs.py, a command-line helper that builds/loads build/GALE01/report.json and lists candidate functions filtered by unit path, function size, virtual address, fuzzy match percentage, and result count. The PR also updated misc Python requirements to include humanfriendly and prettytable, plus their compiled lockfile entries, so the tool can format sizes and print a readable table.
what_changed: The script runs ninja build/GALE01/report.json before reading the report JSON.
Introduced a Function dataclass with name, unit, size, matched, and address fields for candidate results.
Added filters for positional unit paths plus min/max size, min/max virtual address, min/max fuzzy match percent, and max results.
Results are sorted by function size and address, then printed as a PrettyTable with Address, Unit, Function, Size, and Matched columns.
Path input is normalized through PurePosixPath and strips prefixes such as repository root, build/GALE01, src, asm, and leading slash.
Hex address arguments are parsed with optional 0x prefix removal.
Updated reqs/misc.in to include GitPython, humanfriendly, mistletoe, and prettytable.
Regenerated reqs/misc.txt with humanfriendly==10.0, prettytable==3.11.0, and wcwidth==0.2.13 added.
smart_moves: The tool consumes the existing build report instead of reimplementing symbol or map parsing, making it reusable across the current decomp reporting pipeline.
Running ninja for the report path first helps ensure easy_funcs.py works from fresh or stale builds without requiring users to remember a separate report generation step.
Filtering on fuzzy_match_percent defaults to max 0%, which targets completely unmatched functions by default and matches the stated goal of finding easy functions.
Combining size, address, unit, and fuzzy-match filters lets contributors narrow the search to small or localized functions instead of scanning the whole project manually.
Using sanitized unit paths allows users to pass paths in several common forms, including src paths, asm paths, build paths, or absolute repository paths.
Sorting candidates by size then address prioritizes smaller functions, which are often lower-effort decomp targets.
decomp_lessons: For contributor workflow tooling, prefer querying build/GALE01/report.json when possible; it already contains unit names, function names, sizes, fuzzy match percentages, and virtual addresses.
A useful easy-target finder should support both structural filters such as unit path and semantic progress filters such as fuzzy match percentage.
Defaulting to small maximum size and 0% matched makes the tool immediately useful for finding small undecompiled functions without extra flags.
Formatting output as a compact table improves triage speed when selecting functions to attempt next.
When adding Python tooling with new imports, update both reqs/misc.in and the compiled reqs/misc.txt lockfile.
naming_conventions: Script name uses lowercase snake_case: easy_funcs.py.
CLI flags use kebab-case long options such as --min-size, --max-size, --min-virtual-address, --max-virtual-address, --min-matched, --max-matched, and --max-results.
Internal Python identifiers use snake_case, including create_trie, get_report, print_funcs, sanitize_path, sanitize_hex, func_size, func_matched, and func_addr.
The report path constant is named REPORT_PATH and points to build/GALE01/report.json.
The module prefix constant is named MODULE and set to main.
PrettyTable headers use title-case labels: Address, Unit, Function, Size, Matched.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use fuzzy_match_percent from the report to find functions with a chosen match range, especially defaulting to 0% for not-yet-matched functions.
Use size bounds, with a default maximum of 512 bytes, to identify smaller candidate functions likely to be easier decompilation targets.
Use virtual address bounds to restrict the search to a specific address range.
Use unit path filtering to search within a directory or source/asm file area rather than the whole main module.
Sort by size and address to present likely low-complexity candidates first.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that modify tools consuming build/GALE01/report.json.
Find PRs adding scripts for selecting unmatched or fuzzy-matched functions.
Find later changes to tools/easy_funcs.py for bug fixes or option changes.
Find PRs updating reqs/misc.in and reqs/misc.txt for Python tooling dependencies.
